name: Cherries Jubilee Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup kirsch or other cherry liqueur
17 ounce can pitted dark sweet cherries, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. Combine crumbs, 1/4 cup sugar, and margarine. Press into a 9-inch springform pan.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the cheese until fluffy.
4. Gradually beat in sweetened condensed milk, then eggs, 3 tablespoons kirsch, and 1 teaspoon vanilla until smooth.
5. Chop 1/2 cup cherries. Toss with flour.
6. Stir the flour-cherry mixture into the cheese mixture.
7. Pour into pan.
8. Bake for 1 hour and 10 minutes or until set. Cool.
9. Chill.
10. In a saucepan, combine cornstarch, 3 tablespoons sugar, reserved cherry syrup, 1 tablespoon kirsch, and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla. Cook and stir until thickened.
11. Cool.
12. Halve the remaining cherries and add to sauce.
13. Chill.
14. Serve with cheesecake.
15. Refrigerate leftovers.
